theorem: Binary leading forms that are not pure powers of a linear form never yield plane Keller maps in the tested patterns: two-factor monoms x^{d-k}y^k, square-free (x^2-y^2)y^{d-2}, and general binary R with g=y (Keller iff R pure in y). Pure powers work (axis + GL2 conjugates). Proportional leading + free H_{d-1} forces pure y when a=1,b=0 through d<=4.
